title: Star Wars: Rebel Assault II: The Hidden Empire
content:
{{Top|rwm|reb|italics=1}} {{Otheruses|Rebel Assault|more=Star Wars: Hidden Empire}} {{Redlink}} {{VideoGame |image=[[File:RAIIbox.jpg]] |title=''Star Wars: Rebel Assault II:
The Hidden Empire'' |developer=[[LucasArts]] |publisher=LucasArts |release date=[[November]] [[1995]]{{LucasArts|url=20th/history_3.htm|text=20th Anniversary History, Part Three, Broadening Horizons, 1995-1998}} |closed= |upc= |engine=[[INSANE]] |genre=Action ([[Wikipedia:Interactive movie|Interactive movie]]) |modes=[[Wikipedia:Single player|Single player]] |ratings=*[[Wikipedia:ESRB|ESRB]]: Teen (T) *[[Wikipedia:Unterhaltungssoftware Selbstkontrolle|USK]]: 16+{{WebCite|url=http://usk.de/cont3.php?title=rebel+assault+II&s.x=0&s.y=0&s=search|text=rebel+assault+II&s.x=0&s.y=0&s=search|work=[[Wikipedia:Unterhaltungssoftware Selbstkontrolle|USK]]|archivedate=20091220224208}} |platforms=[[Wikipedia:Personal Computer|PC]], [[Wikipedia:Macintosh|Macintosh]], [[Wikipedia:Playstation|Playstation]] |series= |basegame= |expansions= |director= |producer= |designer= |programmer= |artist= |writer= |composer= |timeline=[[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]]''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' |prev=''[[Star Wars: Rebel Assault]]'' |next= }} '''''Star Wars: Rebel Assault II: The Hidden Empire''''' is a [[1995]] video game. It is the sequel to ''[[Star Wars: Rebel Assault]]''. ==Opening crawl== {{OpeningCrawl |name=Episode II
THE HIDDEN EMPIRE |text=By destroying the Death Star, Rebel Forces proved themselves a serious threat to the GALACTIC EMPIRE. Darth Vader, enraged by his defeat, became obsessed with the Alliance's elimination. Toward this end, his forces have scoured the Galaxy for a new weapon; a weapon Vader hopes can catch the Alliance off guard and give the Empire undisputed rule of the Galaxy...}} ==Plot summary== After the destruction of the [[Death Star/Legends|Death Star]], Darth Vader has begun developing a new secret weapon for the Galactic Empire. Meanwhile, in the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]], some rumors of "[[ghost ship]]s" attacking Rebel patrols have spread among the ranks. Rebel pilot [[Kirby]] with his wingman, [[Rookie One]], receive a distress call from a [[Corellia Star|Corellian Transport]], while attacked by [[TIE/ln space superiority starfighter/Legends|TIE Fighters]]. The pilot had crucial information about the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire's]] new project. After the first wave of TIEs, laser shots come from nowhere and shoot Kirby down. Rookie One is left alone and lands on the planet [[Dreighton]] where he goes to rescue the captured transport and escape with it. Back at [[Pinnacle Base]], [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Gial Ackbar/Legends|Gial Ackbar]] helps the pilots understand the message from the freighter. In the Corellian Transport's records, the Alliance learn about a secret Imperial mining facility in the asteroid [[Arah asteroid belt|Belt of Arah]] and a squadron of [[T-65 X-wing starfighter/Legends|X-wings]] moves to destroy it. During that mission, the X-Wing squadron discovers that the Empire was not simply mining ore, but is instead supplying rare metals required to manufacture a stealth version of the TIE Fighter; the new V38 "Phantom" equipped with a [[Cloaking device/Legends|cloaking device]] invented by Grand Admiral Sarn, allowing them to move through space neither spotted on sensors nor by any pilot until they were ready to strike. After destroying the facility, Maarek is killed along with Rookie One's wingman. Rookie One narrowly escapes into hyperspace. Back at Pinnacle Base, Admiral Ackbar comes up with a plan for 2 people to infiltrate the facility building the Phantom P-38s at Imdaar Alpha. Rookie One takes a crash course in TIE piloting from Admiral Krane. Then, they head out to the jump point. Rookie One teams up with [[Ru Murleen]] on the planet [[Imdaar/Legends|Imdaar]]. They fly to an Imperial Landing Platform on the far side of the swamp on Swoop Bikes. Disguised as stormtroopers, they infiltrate the Super Star Destroyer ''[[Terror]]''. Stealing a [[TIE/ph "Phantom" Multi-Role Stealth Starfighter/Legends|TIE Phantom]], they manage to destroy both the ''Terror'' and the [[Imdaar Alpha|TIE Phantom manufacturing plant]]. Though they were able to return the stolen TIE Phantom to a nearby base, the Rebellion was unable to take advantage of the stealth technology, after a self-destruct destroyed their stolen ship. ==Development== This part of the series contained mostly original filming with actors and stunts, while the scenery and the space scenes were 3D-rendered. According to [[LucasArts]]' magazine ''[[The Adventurer]]'', this game was the first media (apart from [[Caravan of Courage: An Ewok Adventure|''Caravan of Courage'']]) to incorporate live-action actors and footage in the ''[[Star Wars]]'' universe since the [[Star Wars: Episode VI Return of the Jedi|''Return of the Jedi'']].[[The Adventurer 10|''The Adventurer'' 10]] In what would foreshadow the filming style of the [[prequel trilogy]], the actors were mainly shot against bluescreens. Most of the environment seen in ''Rebel Assault II'' was computer generated; not even the cockpits of the [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighters]] were constructed sets. Instead, the actors were placed on a bobbing seat-like construct and filmed, while the cockpit (including the proper ship movement) was added in post-production.{{LucasArts|url=products/rebel2/reb_vid.htm|text=''Rebel Assault II'' - Live Action Video}} The [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtrooper]] armor, weapons, helmets and suits, and [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader's]] costume worn by the actors were not made specifically for the game, but were the actual props seen in the original trilogy. They were taken from the archive storage of [[Lucasfilm]], and worn for the first time in 18 years. ==Continuity== While ''[[Star Wars: Rebel Assault]]'' had one portion of gameplay (Rookie One's trench run) deemed as S-[[canon]], its sequel avoided any such difficulties by having a completely original plot. Rookie One's recollections of some of the events of the first two levels of the game appeared in "[[LucasArts Update|Excerpts from the Journal of a Rebel Pilot]]," published in [[Star Wars Insider 27|''Star Wars Insider'' 27]], while the [[TIE/ph "Phantom" Multi-Role Stealth Starfighter/Legends|TIE Phantom]] has been thence adopted to other sources, and described by [[Star Wars Insider 66|''Star Wars Insider'' 66]]. The game also established the player [[Rookie One]] as a male, something its predecessor had left open. He commands ships not appearing in the previous game, like a [[YT-1300 light freighter/Legends|YT-1300 light freighter]], a [[A/SF-01 B-wing starfighter/Legends|B-wing starfighter]] and a [[BTL Y-wing starfighter/Legends|BTL Y-wing starfighter]], and encounters new opponents, like [[TIE/IN interceptor/Legends|TIE/IN interceptors]]. ==Media== {{Mediacat|from=1|imagecat=Images from Star Wars: Rebel Assault II: The Hidden Empire}} ===Cover gallery=== File:RAIIbox.jpg|Cover File:RebAssault2_PSedition.jpg|Special Playstation Edition cover ==Credits== {{Credits |cast= [[File:RookieOne5.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Jamison Jones as Rookie One]] *[[Jamison Jones]]—[[Rookie One]] *[[Julie Eccles]]—[[Ru Murleen]] *[[Gary Martinez]]—[[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Sarn]] *[[C.
